name: cloak
description: MCP-native secrets vault. The model never sees plaintext secrets.
long_description:
Cloak replaces the anti-pattern of pasting API keys into prompts with a hardened local daemon that never exposes raw secret material to the model. Agents call action-shaped MCP tools (sign_request, proxy_authenticated_http_request, mint_short_lived_token) and the daemon performs the privileged operation on the agent's behalf. Reveal is a deliberate biometric-gated CLI act, not a tool call.

This extension bundles the cloak-mcp shim. It requires the cloak CLI + cloakd daemon to be installed separately (see https://cloakward.dev/install). On first activation the extension runs `cloak setup` which walks you through vault init, daemon launch, and biometric/passphrase prompts via your OS-native dialog flow — no terminal commands required.

tools.name: list_secret_names
tools.description: List the names and metadata of secrets stored in the local Cloak vault. Returns names, kinds, and tags only — never the secret values themselves.

tools.name: get_secret_metadata
tools.description: Return metadata about a single named secret (kind, tags, created/updated timestamps, version). Never returns the secret value.

tools.name: sign_request
tools.description: Compute authentication headers for an outbound HTTP request using a stored secret as the signing key. Supports AWS SigV4 and generic HMAC-SHA256. Returns only the computed headers — the underlying secret is never disclosed.

tools.name: proxy_authenticated_http_request
tools.description: Perform an authenticated HTTP request via the daemon, attaching a stored secret as bearer / basic / custom-header / query-param auth. Allowlisted hosts only. Returns the upstream response; the secret never leaves the daemon.

tools.name: mint_short_lived_token
tools.description: Mint a short-lived derived token from a long-lived parent secret (AWS STS session credentials, GitHub App installation token, scoped GitLab PAT). Returns the derived token and its expiry. The long-lived parent never leaves the daemon.

tools.name: query_audit
tools.description: Query the local Cloak audit log of privileged operations. Filterable by time range, tool name, secret name, and result. Returns audit entries — never secret values.
